MINI-MINUTES FOR APR-12-2010 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER MATTHEW KAMELAMELA HEARING OF PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE AND JUDICIAL REVIEW OF ADMINISTRATIVE ORDER. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PETITION DENIED. THE RECORD REFLECTS THAT OFFICER RUECA HAD REASONABLE CAUSE TO BELIEVE THAT PETITIONER WAS DRIVING UNDER THE INFLUENCE. PETITIONER WAS UNDER ARREST AT 3:15 A.M. WHEN OFFICER RUECA READ THE DS367 ADMONITION TO PETITIONER. (SEE PARAGRAPH 4) AFTER HEARING THE ADMONITION, A REASONABLE PERSON WOULD BELIEVE HE WAS IN CUSTODY. OFFICER RUECA DESCRIBED HOW PETITIONER AGREED TO THE BLOOD TEST, BUT AFTERWARD BECAME OBSTREPEROUS AND MOVED AWAY FROM THE PHLEBOTOMIST, REFUSING THE TEST. NO COMPELLING EVIDENCE SUPPORTS HEAD TRAUMA AS A REASON FOR PETITIONER'S ACTIONS. (ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD, LODGED BY COUNSEL FOR RESPONDENT, IS RETURNED TO COUNSEL FOR RESPONDENT AT TODAY'S HEARING.) (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing Of Petition For Writ Of Mandate And Judicial Review Of Administrative Order;
Off Calendar Apr-22-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER MATTHEW KAMELAMELA Hearing of Petition for Writ of Mandate and Judicial Review of Administrative Order. Appearances: Tina Poley, Esq. of Keith Staten & Associates (916) 443-5600 Appearing thru CourtCall for Petitioner and moving party; Lyn Harlan, Esq. Deputy Attorney General, (510) 622-2208 appearing for Respondent and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Petition denied. The record reflects that Officer Rueca had reasonable cause to believe that petitioner was driving under the influence. Petitioner was under arrest at 3:15 a.m. when Officer Rueca read the DS367 admonition to Petitioner. (See paragraph 4) After hearing the admonition, a reasonable person would believe he was in custody. Officer Rueca described how petitioner agreed to the blood test, but afterward became obstreperous and moved away from the phlebotomist, refusing the test. No compelling evidence supports head trauma as a reason for petitioner's actions. (Administrative record, lodged by counsel for respondent, is returned to counsel for respondent at today's hearing.) (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
